tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and mild ketonuria, with abnormal urine appearance and color, but no evidence of infection. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria. Findings are consistent with possible renal dysfunction (e.g., nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy), but the low HbA1c warrants further investigation. Recommend nephrology referral and assessment for possible laboratory interference or hemoglobinopathy affecting HbA1c.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
